reason: Measurement timing fix confirmed working: winner_margin_inference=+0.0077 now accurately reflects the saved model (iter-40 best-ckpt). Hard gates PASS (Part B ok). +0.0077 exceeds old v07d7 research baseline (+0.0059) but is below current v07d11 research baseline (+0.0080) by 0.0003 — within run variance. The iter-40 peak is consistent across v07d11 (+0.0080) and v07d12 (+0.0077), confirming this is a stable checkpoint location. Research baseline remains at +0.0080 (v07d11). Next aggressive experiment should extend N_ITERS or warm-start from v07d11/v07d12 model.
artifact_reuse: v06d18 IL weights ([:,:96]); v06d18 feature matrix ([:,:96])

research_baseline.note: Unchanged — v07d12 (+0.0077) did not beat v07d11 (+0.0080)

key_findings.measurement_fix: winner_margin_inference now computed AFTER best-ckpt restore — accurate
key_findings.iter40_consistency: iter-40 is best checkpoint in BOTH v07d11 (+0.0080) and v07d12 (+0.0077). The peak is consistently around iter 40 with 50-iter training.
key_findings.peak_pattern: iters 0-30 always negative (policy diverges from IL), iter 40 peak, iters 40-49 decline. Suggests optimal checkpoint is near iter 40.
key_findings.run_variance: +0.0080 vs +0.0077 across identical runs — ~0.0003 variance is small and acceptable.

next_candidates: [aggressive] v07d13: extend N_ITERS=100 (same hyperparams). iter-40 is consistently best with 50 iters; more iters may find higher peak or confirm iter-40 is the global optimum.
[aggressive] v07d13-warmstart: warm-start from v07d12 model, run 50 more iters. May find higher quality since starting from +0.0077 instead of IL baseline.
[conservative] v07d13-freq: reduce checkpoint interval to every 5 iters (not 10) for finer best-ckpt selection around iter 40.
[aggressive] v07d13-lambda: reduce lambda_il=0.4 (slight reduction from 0.5) to give RL more freedom while maintaining IL anchor.
